events.title: Israel-Hezbollah Clashes Escalate
events.desc: Following a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, Israel has intensified strikes on Lebanon, resulting in over 160 fatalities since Friday. Hezbollah's attacks continue, indicating a persistent and volatile conflict on this front despite broader regional de-escalation efforts.

events.title: Palestinian Killed in West Bank Settler Attack
events.desc: A Palestinian was killed in an occupied West Bank village during an attack by Israeli settlers. This incident occurs amid warnings from ex-security chiefs about 'government-sponsored Jewish terrorism' being out of control, highlighting ongoing violence and instability in the region.

events.title: US Blockades Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US has initiated a military blockade of Iran's ports and the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to cut off approximately 2 million barrels per day of Iranian crude exports. This action raises concerns about potential energy shocks and further exacerbates regional tensions.

events.title: US-Iran Talks Conclude Without Breakthrough
events.desc: High-level talks between US and Iranian officials in Islamabad have concluded without a definitive breakthrough after extensive negotiations. However, sources indicate that dialogue channels remain open, suggesting potential for future engagement.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been leaked online, exposing basic security vulnerabilities. The breach impacts 12 out of 13 ministries, potentially compromising sensitive and classified information.

events.title: Iran-linked Drone Strikes on Saudi Arabia
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles targeted Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. This follows earlier attacks on the same base within two weeks, indicating a pattern of asymmetric counterair operations against US forces in the region.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Deploys Mines Over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence suggests the US deployed Gator Scatterable Mine systems over Kafari, a village near Shiraz in southern Iran, leading to multiple casualties according to Iranian media. Experts identified the munitions as US-made Gator anti-tank mines, indicating their use in the ongoing conflict.

events.title: Iran Imposes Internet Blackout
events.desc: During recent strikes, the Iranian government deliberately halted internet traffic, causing a 98% collapse in connectivity. This action was imposed on the Iranian populace, distinct from the attacks on military and leadership targets by adversaries.

events.title: Israel Kills Palestinians in Gaza Strikes
events.desc: Two Palestinians were killed in separate strikes in southern Gaza. Officials confirmed the casualties at Nasser hospital, indicating ongoing kinetic actions in the region.

events.title: Philippines Accuses China of Poisoning South China Sea
events.desc: The Philippines has accused China of using cyanide to poison an atoll in the South China Sea. Officials warn that this action could harm marine life and weaken the reef supporting a grounded warship.

events.title: Hezbollah Rejects Lebanon-Israel Negotiations
events.desc: Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem urged the Lebanese government to cancel direct negotiations with Israel planned in Washington. He stated that Hezbollah would continue to confront Israeli attacks, signaling strong opposition to direct diplomatic engagement.

events.title: Lafarge Found Guilty of Funding Terrorism in Syria
events.desc: A French court has ruled that the cement giant Lafarge is guilty of financing terrorist groups, including IS, in Syria. The company is ordered to pay a fine, and executives face jail time for their actions amid the Syrian civil war.
